version_reason: Remove reviewer-owned semantic centrality and the hidden exactly-one-central cardinality contract.
purpose: Independently identify Atomic Claim boundaries and structural metadata while protocol-owned normalization assigns structural roles.
unit_of_review: one source anchor within one frozen real-provider Candidate
anchor_group_rule: case_id + '::' + source_field; all Atomic Claims split from the same structural field share a protocol-owned group.
cardinality_rules: Every source anchor must contain at least one Atomic Claim.
One source anchor may contain multiple non-overlapping Atomic Claims.
A proposition anchor may contain multiple anchor-role Atomic Claims.
No exactly-one-central or exactly-one-anchor-claim constraint exists.
span_semantics: UTF-8 decoded Python string code-point offsets [start,end) within exactly one source_text anchor.
boundary_rules: Every independently truth-evaluable assertion must receive its own non-empty span.
Claims may not cross source-anchor boundaries.
Claims may not overlap within an anchor.
Do not assign support labels or evidence IDs.
Do not assign centrality, claim_role, or anchor_group.
Claim origin is structural provenance only and is not a support decision.
freeze_rule: Boundary Gold may freeze only after two completed blind submissions, immutable Agreement, completed adjudication, and passing Boundary Coverage QA.
